title: Direct Filing Addresses for Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker | USCIS
content:
## Direct Filing Addresses for Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker
Alert: Before sending your package to USCIS, make sure that all the forms you have completed are the current acceptable edition. The current acceptable edition can be found under the Edition Date section listed on each form’s webpage.
You can also find the edition date at the bottom of the page on the form and instructions. For each form that you are submitting, make sure that all pages are from the same form edition. If any of the form’s pages are from a different form edition, we may reject your form. Watch USCIS Forms: Download the Most Recent Version! to see how to ensure your form is acceptable.
For additional guidance on filing paper-based forms at the Lockbox and how to avoid or correct common errors before you file, please visit the Five Steps to File at the USCIS Lockbox webpage.
All Forms I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker , are filed at a USCIS lockbox facility. Please see the “I-129 Nonimmigrant Classification Filing Locations” accordions below for the proper filing location for each Form I-129 nonimmigrant classification category.
The “I-129 Nonimmigrant Classification Filing Locations” accordions contain information regarding the proper filing location for each nonimmigrant classification and requested action that may be submitted using Form I-129. If you file your petition at the incorrect location, your petition may be rejected.

## Listing Your Organization’s Mailing Address
Your company’s or organization’s primary office in the United States should be listed in Part 1, Question 3.
For certain H-1B petitions, the U.S. state or territory where your company or organization’s primary office is located will determine where you should file your Form I-129 package. Other H-1B and H-1B1 (HSC) petitions are filed at only one lockbox location, regardless of where your company or organization’s primary office is located.

## Providing Additional Information
In addition to Part 5, Question 3, you may also use Part 9 of Form I-129 to list additional worksite locations if you need extra space.
If you are requesting premium processing services for a Form I-129, you must also file Form I-907, Request for Premium Processing Service . The filing locations for Form I-907 associated with Form I-129 are listed in the “I-129 Nonimmigrant Classification Filing Locations” accordions below. Before you file the Form I-129/I-907 package, check our How Do I Request Premium Processing? page to ensure that the requested classification is eligible for premium processing.
If you wish to file a standalone Form I-907 to upgrade a pending Form I-129 after we have transferred your Form I-129 petition to a different location and you received a transfer notice,  see the "Form I-907 to Upgrade a Pending Form I-129 to Premium Processing" accordion below for information on where to submit your Form I-907.  You should also include a copy of the Form I-129 transfer notice with your premium processing request to avoid delays.

#### Form I-907 to Upgrade a Pending Form I-129 to Premium Processing
Use these filing locations when filing a standalone Form I-907, Request for Premium Processing Service , to upgrade a pending Form I-129 for the eligible I-129 filing classifications listed below.
- E-1 and E-2 applications filed by an employer requesting an extension of stay or change of status;
- E-2C (CNMI) applications filed by an employer requesting an extension of stay;
- E-3 filings seeking an extension of stay or change of status for a worker;
- H-1B, H-1B2, and H-1B3 classifications;
- H-2B petitions where the beneficiary will be employed as a temporary non-agricultural worker;
- H-2B petitions where the beneficiary will be performing labor or services in Guam;
- H-3 petitions filed by an employer for a beneficiary who is a filing location.
- L-1A, L-1B, and LZ (Blanket L-1) classifications;
- O-1A, O-1B, and O-2 classifications;
- P-1A or P-1S major league sports organization petitions (NHL, MLB, NFL, NBA and MLS only);
- P-1A athlete or athletic group other than major league sports, P-1B, P-1S, P-2, P-2S, P-3 and P-3S;
- Q-1 petitions filed by an employer for a beneficiary who will participate in an international cultural exchange program;
- R-1 nonimmigrant classification for an alien who will be employed as a minister or in a religious occupation or vocation; and
- TN-1 or TN-2 petitions requesting an extension of stay or change of status, or a TN-1 petition filed on behalf of a Canadian citizen who is outside of the United States.
We will reject any standalone Form I-907 that is not filed at the correct filing location. Do not resubmit Form I-129.
